name: Burgui by Salvatierra de Esca — round trip from Artieda
name_original: Burgui por Salvatierra de Esca — ida y vuelta desde Artieda
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Artieda are situated within the Pyrenean foothills, offering diverse terrain and mountain views. The region is characterized by the Aragon River valley and proximity to the Yesa Reservoir, a significant natural feature. Cyclists encounter varied elevations, from smoother gradients along river paths to significant climbs in the highlands. This area provides a blend of natural beauty and challenging routes for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Artieda The most popular road cycling route is Berdún - Bailo - Bagü, a 50.0 miles (80.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 59 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Pyrenean foothills. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Burgui by Salvatierra de Esca — round trip from Artieda, a moderate 27.2 miles (43.8 km) path. This route offers scenic views around Artieda and the surrounding landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Ascent to Lorbés — round trip from the abandoned village of Ruesta, a 37.9 miles (61.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with an ascent, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Road cycling around Artieda is defined by Pyrenean foothills, the Aragon River valley, and the Yesa Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Artieda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Artieda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Artieda?
A: Artieda offers a diverse range of road cycling routes, from moderate rides with scenic views to challenging routes featuring significant climbs through the Pyrenean foothills. You'll encounter varied terrain, including smoother gradients along the Aragon River valley and more demanding ascents in the highlands.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Artieda is known for its challenging routes with substantial elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992897367/berdun-bailo-bague" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berdún - Bailo - Bagü&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 80.6 km (50.0 miles) trail with over 1190 meters of elevation gain, taking you through the Pyrenean foothills. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40377498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pierre Saint-Martin Pass – Col de la Pierre Saint-Martin loop from Salvatierra de Esca&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 105 km with more than 1670 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features or scenic viewpoints can I expect to see while cycling?
A: Road cycling routes around Artieda offer stunning views of the Pyrenean foothills and the Aragon River valley. Many routes also provide picturesque vistas of the Yesa Reservoir, often referred to as the 'Sea of the Pyrenees'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5333389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berdún Hilltop Village&lt;/a&gt; is another notable viewpoint along some routes, offering impressive panoramic scenery.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical landmarks along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Artieda is situated on the Aragonese Route of the Camino de Santiago, so you might encounter sections of this historic pilgrimage path. The region is rich in Romanesque architecture, with charming villages and ancient structures. You can also visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5266483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruesta Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the medieval character of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5258574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artieda itself&lt;/a&gt;, which features traditional mountain architecture and Renaissance buildings.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Artieda?
A: Given Artieda's location in the Pyrenean foothills, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures. Summer can also be suitable, especially for routes at higher altitudes, but be prepared for warmer weather. Winters can be cold, and some higher passes might be inaccessible due to snow.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes around Artieda are designed as circular trips, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992892506/burgui-by-salvatierra-de-esca-round-trip-from-artieda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgui by Salvatierra de Esca — round trip from Artieda&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views and brings you back to your starting point.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Artieda?
A: The road cycling routes in Artieda are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from numerous reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the stunning Pyrenean landscapes, and the tranquil roads that allow for an immersive experience through unspoiled nature.

Q: Are there any moderate-difficulty routes suitable for a scenic ride?
A: Certainly. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992883853/ascent-to-lorbes-round-trip-from-the-abandoned-village-of-ruesta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Lorbés — round trip from the abandoned village of Ruesta&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 61.0 km (37.9 miles) trail that takes you through varied terrain with an ascent, offering a rewarding experience without being overly strenuous. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992892506/burgui-by-salvatierra-de-esca-round-trip-from-artieda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgui by Salvatierra de Esca — round trip from Artieda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other outdoor activities in the Artieda area?
A: Yes, Artieda serves as an excellent hub for various outdoor activities. Beyond road cycling, you can enjoy hiking, mountain biking, fishing, and water activities in the Aragon River or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2001608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yesa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. This makes it ideal for cyclists who wish to diversify their outdoor adventures.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes near Artieda?
A: For those seeking longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40314301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Xavier – Yesa Reservoir loop from Salvatierra de Esca&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial ride of nearly 98 km (60.8 miles) with significant elevation, providing a full day's challenge and exploration of the wider region.

Q: Where can I find information about parking for road cycling routes in Artieda?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed here, Artieda and nearby villages typically offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival or consult local tourist information for the most convenient parking spots near your chosen starting point.
tips: Beautiful road that runs through the spectacular Foz de Burgui Nature Reserve
Burgi (or Burgui) is a beautiful and picturesque town located on the banks of the Ezka River. It is the ideal place to start your journey through this area and reach the beautiful Foz de Burgui.

waypoints.name: Views of the Foz de Burgui
